column8: In 1990, there were fewer than 300 Siberian tigers in the world.
column9: In Russia, a person has the right to hunt a Siberian tiger.
column10: A lot of Siberian tigers were killed in a battle near Vladivostok.
column11: The Siberian Tiger Project was only concerned with stopping illegal hunting.
column12: North Korea participates in conservation efforts.
column13: Both Russia and China tell citizens about the problems Siberian tigers experience.
column14: Siberian tigers could live with other tigers if they had enough space.
column15: More Siberian tigers live in zoos than anywhere else.
